parts.answer: The only correct answer is B ( ) A is incorrect because the electrophile oxygen atom does not have a negative charge and has a double bond to the central carbon C is incorrect because the positive charge should be on the central carbon D is incorrect because the central carbon does not have a lone pair of electrons (1) Number Answer Mark

The only correct answer is B ( ) A is incorrect because the correct structure does not have a complete delocalised ring of electrons C is incorrect because the correct structure does not have a positive charge on the tetrahedral carbon atom D is incorrect because the ‘horseshoe’ of delocalised electrons should include the five carbon atoms of the ring excluding the tetrahedral carbon atom (1) Number Answer Mark A is incorrect because this is the value calculated when one electron is transferred instead of two C is incorrect because this is the value calculated when one electron is transferred instead of two and the temperature value used is in degrees Celsius not Kelvin D is incorrect because this is the value calculated when the temperature value used is in degrees Celsius not Kelvin (1) Number Answer Mark ) B is incorrect because this is the value of K calculated on the basis of one electron transferred instead of two C is incorrect because this is the value of ln K D is incorrect because this is the value of ln K calculated on the basis of one electron transferred instead of two (1) Number Answer Mark A is incorrect because electrons do not flow through the membrane B is incorrect because electrons do not flow through the membrane and they move from the negative electrode to the positive electrode D is incorrect because electrons move from the negative to the positive electrode (1) Number Answer Mark

The only correct answer is A (H (g) + 2OH − (aq) ⇌ 2H O(l) + 2e − ) B is incorrect because this equation shows the simultaneous reduction of hydrogen and the oxidation of oxygen C is incorrect because this equation shows the simultaneous reduction of hydrogen and the oxidation of oxygen D is incorrect because this is the oxidation equation for the fuel cell with an acidic electrolyte (1) Number Answer Mark A is incorrect because this is the percentage by mass of hydrogen in water B is incorrect because this is the percentage using atomic numbers C is incorrect because this is the percentage using atomic numbers and doubling it (1) Number Answer Mark 7 The only correct answer is A (chromium and copper) B is incorrect because manganese does follow the expected pattern with 3d for the fifth element in the d-block C is incorrect because scandium does follow the expected pattern with 3d for the first element in the d-block D is incorrect because scandium does follow the expected pattern with 3d for the first element in the d-block and so does zinc with 3d for the tenth element in the d-block (1) Number Answer Mark 8 The only correct answer is C (ligand exchange) A is incorrect because deprotonation involves loss of hydrogen ions B is incorrect because the carbon monoxide is replacing the oxygen molecule in the complex D is incorrect because oxygen is being replaced by the carbon monoxide but this is not the best description (1) Number Answer Mark B is incorrect because the lack of reaction with carbonates and water means that there are no carboxylic acid or acyl chloride groups which are required for condensation polymerisation C is incorrect because the lack of reaction with carbonates and water means that there are no carboxylic acid or acyl chloride groups which are required for condensation polymerisation D is incorrect because the ability to react by electrophilic addition indicates the presence of a carbon-carbon double bond which can undergo
